LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TOM UNGER & STEPHANIE LOSEE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH, AND WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION AND ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS. GIVEN THE REQUIREMENT TO ACCEPT AS TRUE THE PLEADED FACTS, NO MATTER HOW IMPROBABLE, PLAINTIFFS HAVE SUFFICIENTLY PLEADED ALTER EGO. FOR THE FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS HAVE SUFFICIENTLY PLED FACTS TO ALLEGE BREACH OF CONTRACT AND LABOR CODE VIOLATIONS CLAIMS UNDER THE ALTER EGO DOCTRINE. "THE ESSENCE OF THE ALTER EGO DOCTRINE IS NOT THAT THE INDIVIDUAL SHAREHOLDER BECOMES THE CORPORATION, BUT THAT THE INDIVIDUAL SHAREHOLDER IS LIABLE FOR THE ACTIONS OF THE CORPORATION." (LEEK V. COOPER (2011) 194 CAL.APP.4TH 399, 415.) FOR THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. UNGER CANNOT CONSPIRE WITH UNIVERSITY PIPELINE, INC. WHERE ALTER EGO LIABILITY IS ALLEGED. (APPLIED EQUIPMENT CORP. V. LITTON SAUDI ARABIA LTD. (1994) 7 CAL.4TH 503, 512, FN. 4.) EVEN ALTERNATIVELY, HE COULD NOT CONSPIRE WITH HIS EMPLOYER. FOR THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE CONVERSION OF PROPERTY OTHER THAN UNPAID WAGES. (THOMAS V. HOME DEPOT USA INC. (N.D. CAL. 2007) 527 F.SUPP.2D 1003, 1010.) FOR THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THOUGH PLAINTIFFS MAY PLEAD INCONSISTENT LEGAL THEORIES, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PLEAD THE NONEXISTENCE OR NONENFORCEABILITY OF THE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT. (KLEIN V. CHEVRON U.S.A., INC. (2012) 202 CAL.APP.4TH 1342, 1389-90.) THE PLEADINGS ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Tom Unger'S Supplemental Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories To Defendant Tom Unger, Set One, And For Sanctions.
Off Calendar Mar-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Douglas Akay, Esq., 415.764.1999, for Plaintiff Ryan Garman.
Daniel Croley, Esq., 415.399.3840, for Defendant Tom Unger.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: PLAINTIFF RYAN GARMAN'S Motion To Compel Defendant Tom Unger'S Supplemental Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories To Defendant Tom Unger, Set One, And For Sanctions.

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories - Set One is denied. Defendant's objection that the Motion to Compel was untimely filed is overruled. Defendant served his Second Amended Response to Interrogatories during the pendency of an earlier Motion to Compel regarding the same discovery pleading. (See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 8 Cal.App.4th 390. The Court's Order dated January 8, 2014 permitted late service of the Second Amended Response and also directed plaintiff to file a second Motion to Compel if a ruling as to adequacy was needed. Thus, the motion was timely filed within 45 days thereafter.
Interrogatories nos. 1, 6-10, 13-17, 20-24, 27-31, 34-39, 41-60, 63-64, 67-68, 76-77, 80-81, 84, and 115 are overbroad, burdensome and oppressive. It is an unreasonable burden and oppressive to require the Defendant to "IDENTIFY" and/or "DESCRIBE" each and every bank transaction for the years requested. (See Mead Reinsurance Co. v. Sup.Ct. (1986) 188 Cal.App.3d 313.)
For Interrogatories nos. 117, 119-120, 122-14, 161-163, Defendant has affirmed providing all information to the best of his knowledge and in his direct care, custody and control, directly, or in the form of Electronically Stored Information.
Plaintiff's motion to compel Defendant to obtain corporate bank records is moot based on the finding that the underlying inquiries are burdensome and oppressive. Further, the corporate bank records were the subject of subpoenas issued by the Plaintiff, and the records were obtained without objection.
Monetary sanctions in the amount of $2,750.00 are awarded in favor of University Pipeline, Inc. and against Plaintiff's counsel on the ground filing of this motion to compel was not justified to be paid within 20 days of service of this Order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFFS RYAN GARMAN AND GARMAN GROUP, LLC'S Motion To Compel Deft Tom Unger Supplemental Responses And Compliance With Pltfs' Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, And Deft Stephanie Losee'S Supplemental Responses And Compliance With Pltfs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And For Sanctions GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.
(1) DEFENDANTS UNGER AND LOSEE WILL FILE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO ALL REQUESTS WHEREIN THEY RESPONDED WITH GENERAL OR SPECIFIC OBJECTIONS, BUT INDICATED: (A) A WILLINGNESS TO PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS; OR (B) THAT NO RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS EXIST. DEFENDANTS SHALL SPECIFICALLY STATE WHETHER OR NOT THEY ARE WITHHOLDING PRODUCTION OF ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS BASED UPON THESE OBJECTIONS. TO THE EXTENT THEY ARE, THEY WILL IDENTIFY SUCH DOCUMENTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW GARMAN TO FILE A MOTION CHALLENGING THE PROPRIETY OF WITHHOLDING SUCH DOCUMENTS.
(2) THE PARTIES HAVE INDICATED A WILLINGNESS TO HAVE A CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT ENTERED INTO TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY PARTY BELIEVES THAT DOCUMENTS CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NOT SUBJECT TO PUBLIC DISCLOSURE. IT APPEARS FROM THE MATERIALS SUBMITTED TO THE COURT THAT THE PARTIES ARE FAIRLY CLOSE TO AN AGREEMENT ON THE FORM OF SUCH AN ORDER; TO THE EXTENT THAT IT CANNOT BE RESOLVED BY THE PARTIES THE COURT IS PREPARED TO CONSIDER AND ENTER A CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER SO THAT PRODUCTION CAN COMMENCE WITHIN 10 DAYS AFTER ENTRY OF THIS ORDER.
(3) DEFENDANT UNGER'S OBJECTIONS TO SET ONE, REQUEST NOS. 14, 15, 16, 40, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, 48, 54 & 55 AND SET TWO, REQUEST NOS. 90 -97 ARE SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT LOSEE'S OBJECTIONS TO THE SET TWO, REQUEST NOS. 90-97 ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARISE OUT OF HIS RELATIONSHIP WITH THE CORPORATE DEFENDANT AND BASED ON THE RECORD TO DATE THERE IS AN INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO INVADE DEFENDANTS' PRIVACY RIGHTS GUARANTEED BY CALIFORNIA LAW. MERE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE NOT A SUFFICIENT BASIS FOR REQUIRING THE REQUESTED PRODUCTION.
(4) MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $2, 500.00 ARE ASSESSED AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR ATTORNEY TO BE PAID TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Paul Renne, Judge Pro-Tem, Cynthia Herbert, Clerk, Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS TOM UNGER & STEPHANIE LOSEE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained, without leave to amend as to the fifth, and with leave to amend as to the sixth and seventh causes of action, and overruled as to the remaining causes of action and alter ego allegations. Given the requirement to accept as true the pleaded facts, no matter how improbable, Plaintiffs have sufficiently pleaded alter ego. For the first, second and third causes of action, Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled facts to allege breach of contract and Labor Code violations claims under the alter ego doctrine. "The essence of the alter ego doctrine is not that the individual shareholder becomes the corporation, but that the individual shareholder is liable for the actions of the corporation." (Leek v. Cooper (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 399, 415.) For the fifth cause of action, Mr. Unger cannot conspire with University Pipeline, Inc. where alter ego liability is alleged. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 512, fn. 4.) Even alternatively, he could not conspire with his employer. For the sixth cause of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to allege conversion of property other than unpaid wages. (Thomas v. Home Depot USA Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2007) 527 F.Supp.2d 1003, 1010.) For the seventh cause of action, though Plaintiffs may plead inconsistent legal theories, Plaintiff does not plead the nonexistence or nonenforceability of the employment agreement. (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1389-90.) The pleadings are not uncertain. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
